/.homeExample:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <title>Valyrian.js App</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <script type="module">
      import "https://unpkg.com/valyrian.js";

      const { mount } = Valyrian;

      function App() {
        return "Hello from Valyrian.js";
      }

      mount("body", App);
    </script>
  </body>
</html>

/learn.markdown:
# Learn Valyrian.js

Follow a complete path or open the guide that matches the feature you need. Start with views and mounting, then add routing, data, state, offline behavior or server rendering as your application requires.
/learn.title: Learn Valyrian.js

/reference.markdown:
# API reference

Find Valyrian.js modules and symbols by capability. Each entry provides its import, signature, result, errors and related guide.
/reference.title: API reference

/guides.description: Learn each public capability through its purpose, main APIs and place in an application flow. Every guide points to a working example when one is available and continues into the related API reference.

hubGroups.title: Views and lifecycle
hubGroups.introduction: Build vnodes, mount an application and respond to lifecycle changes.

definitions.description: Creates the vnode description that Valyrian.js renders as part of a view.
Connects a root view to a supported target and starts its runtime lifecycle.
Removes the mounted root and ends its mounted lifecycle.

sections.body: `mount` requires a valid target. Use `unmount` to remove the mounted root.
sections.heading: Limit

items.summary: Create a view with `v`, attach it with `mount` and remove the mounted root with `unmount`.
items.introduction: `v` creates the vnode to render. `mount` attaches the root view. `unmount` removes it and runs registered cleanup.
items.title: Build your first Valyrian.js application

definitions.description: Creates the vnode description that Valyrian.js renders as part of a view.
Exposes the registered runtime directives by name.
Renders the supplied HTML string without validating or sanitizing it.

items.summary: Build a view from vnodes, attributes and child values. Use public vnode utilities when you need to inspect or transform supported vnode structures, and use directives when behavior belongs to the rendered node lifecycle.
items.introduction: trust marks content for trusted rendering. Use it only with content whose origin and transformation you control. It does not validate or sanitize untrusted input.
items.title: Compose views and control rendered content

definitions.description: Registers work for the moment a view enters the rendered tree.
